hypotheses.description: High conviction signals (WR=33%) don't outperform low conviction (WR=43%). Conviction feature may not be predictive — consider reweighting or replacing.
hypotheses.hypothesis: Conviction score not predictive — may need recalibration
Types 3 (SD_BAND, 49.7% WR) and 5 (EMA_CROSS, 39.2% WR) have negative OOS edge — removing them from training may improve meta-ensemble by reducing noise
hypotheses.expected_impact: higher meta AUC, fewer noisy L1 features

hypotheses.hypothesis: Type 1 (ORB) has the best in-sample AUC (0.879) but 0 OOS passes at threshold 0.35 — the model may be overfit to pre-2025 ORB patterns. Retrain with 2025-only data weighted 5x.
CB (CatBoost) was set to 0.5 neutral in stacking due to inference bug — fixing CB inference and retraining meta-ensemble with proper CB L1 features should improve meta AUC beyond 0.843
hypotheses.fix: save CB models with joblib.dump (not save_model) OR use Pool with int-encoded cat cols

hypotheses.hypothesis: Threshold 0.50 vs 0.35 tradeoff — 0.35 gives 645 signals/day at 70.1% WR, 0.50 gives 538 signals at 75.3%. For the bot (9 instruments, ~5-15 actual signals/day), higher threshold = higher precision. Test 0.525 as optimal bot threshold.

LSTM was skipped for types 8, 15, 16 (>100K samples) but these are the highest-volume types. Train LSTM on a 50K random subsample of these types — may capture sequential patterns the tabular models miss.
hypotheses.expected_impact: add LSTM signal for 57K+ daily OOS passes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Type 4 (ORB_RETEST, 54.4% WR) is borderline — check if adding orb_direction_bias and 4H trend features improves OOS WR above 60%
Monthly WR ranges 67-74% — Dec 2025 and Jan 2026 are weakest months. Check if macro_risk_score or vix_regime features explain the dip and can be used to reduce position size in those regimes.
Gap_reversal (type 27) has 83.3% WR but only 90 OOS trades — tiny sample. Need 6 more months of live data before trusting this type. Flag for monitoring.

PH2.5 Inside Bar fires on calendar blackout days despite ORB being suppressed. Today (news day): 4L/1W/1T = 33% WR vs expected ~56%. Inside Bar should also be suspended on high-impact news days (NFP/CPI/FOMC) since the volatility regime invalidates the low-range assumption.
hypotheses.expected_impact: reduce losses on news days, may miss some wins

hypotheses.hypothesis: All 5 RTH PH2.5 trades today were LONG despite a bearish news-driven session. A pre-news directional drift filter (e.g. 30-min price vs VWAP before blackout window) could bias PH2.5 direction and avoid counter-trend entries.
hypotheses.expected_impact: filter counter-trend IB entries on news days

hypotheses.hypothesis: Low conviction (0.45-0.60) Inside Bar trades on news-adjacent sessions (calendar blackout active) have consistently poor outcomes. Add minimum conviction floor of 0.65 when calendar_blackout=True to reduce noise trades.
Type 2 (VWAP_REV) AUC=0.5821 in V7 is near-noise and should be masked in V8, same treatment as types 3/5. Masking it should improve meta AUC by removing a noise column from the stacker.
LSTM L1 features are all 0.5 neutral at inference (pipeline broken). Removing LSTM columns from meta stacker input should reduce noise and improve meta AUC without LSTM retraining.
Type 27 (GAP_REVERSAL) achieved AUC=0.8972 in V7 — the strongest new signal. Deploy it as a standalone high-confidence gate at threshold 0.80 without waiting for full V8 meta rebuild.

macro_bars context was None during RTH (detect_bond_stress / detect_dollar_strength crash). ZN and DX bar subscriptions may not be initializing at startup. Adding explicit null-guard is a stopgap — root fix is ensuring macro bar subscriptions succeed on bot start.

Meta calibration leakage: calibration step fits on meta training data (86k OOS samples) and reports AUC on same data → artificially inflated calibrated AUC (0.9880) and Sharpe (400+). Fix: (1) calibrate on val set only, (2) train meta stacker on out-of-fold L1 predictions from pre-OOS period, not on OOS-period predictions, (3) evaluate threshold table on a separate held-out OOS slice not used in meta training.

hypotheses.description: Ph8 CHART_PATTERN signals (Triangle, Engulfing, BB_Squeeze) went 3/3 today with strong P&L. Hypothesis: increase conviction sizing multiplier for CHART_PATTERN bucket by 1.25x when 4H trend aligns — these patterns show directional discipline.

Overnight ORB (ASIAN_CONT/LONDON_ORB) live WR=25%, net=-$315.62 across 8 labels. Primary exit reason: TIME_EXIT. Hypothesis: reduce overnight ORB max_hold from 75min to 30min to cut losers earlier — these markets move in the first window or not at all.

BB_Squeeze (Ph8_BB_SQUEEZE) fired with adx_regime=false but won +$543. Hypothesis: relax ADX gate for BB_Squeeze specifically — squeeze patterns have their own vol confirmation via band width, ADX filter is redundant and may be blocking good signals.

All 4 RTH winners today were Shorts — 4H trend was bearish and all Ph8 patterns aligned with it. Hypothesis: when 4H trend + 3+ Ph8 signals agree on direction within same hour, treat as high-conviction regime and allow up to 3 simultaneous positions instead of 2.

live_labels.jsonl only captures type=1 ORB signals, missing CHART_PATTERN, HF_MR, MOMENTUM buckets which drove all 4 RTH wins today (+$2,231). Fix label writer to log all trade buckets — necessary for accurate live WR tracking and ML feedback loop.

VWAP_REV Ph8 signals fired 6 times in first 10 min of RTH today (9:45-9:55 ET), all Long, all lost. 4H bias was neutral and RSI not aligned on 5/6 trades. Hypothesis: VWAP_REV should require RSI aligned AND 4H trend bullish (not just neutral) before entry. Neutral 4H = skip.

signal_type in live_labels.jsonl still shows type=1 for Ph8_VWAP_REV trades even after da0e59d fix. signal_type_from_phase("Ph8_VWAP_REV") should return 2 but returns 1. Possible: bot.py still passing raw strategy tag rather than phase string, or not calling updated function. Needs investigation.

All 6 losing trades today used conviction=0.6 despite RSI not aligned and ADX regime false. Conviction multiplier should be reduced (0.4-0.5x) when 2+ gate filters fail at entry. Currently only reduces on volatility/time-of-day, not on gate miss count.

All CatBoost models fail in meta L1 prediction with: "data is numpy array of floating point numerical type, it means no categorical features, but cat_features parameter specifies nonzero number". Fix: remove cat_features param when predicting on numpy arrays in inference_v6.py / train_pipeline_v8.py meta stacking step. This dropped all CB contributions from V8 meta, degrading AUC from expected ~0.80 to 0.6252.

tp_mult=3.0 promoted tonight (PF 18.80 >> 16.29). Previous champion used tp_mult=2.5. Suggests market structure supports wider targets. Hypothesis: explore tp_mult=3.5 and 4.0 to find ceiling, especially for chart pattern types (7,8,9) which have longer follow-through.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 5-min per-instrument+direction cooldown after VWAP thesis fail reduces drawdown without materially reducing win count. Validate: compare 5-min vs 10-min vs 15-min cooldown on backtest WR/PF.

VWAP_REV (type 2) threshold 0.60 per-type was being bypassed — all signals used global 0.425. Now fixed. Hypothesis: with correct 0.60 threshold, VWAP_REV WR should recover from ~30% toward 60%+. Monitor next 20 live trades.

VWAP thesis fail should trigger not just a cooldown but also a market regime tag — if thesis fails 2+ times in same direction on same instrument within 30 min, mark that instrument as 'choppy' and skip for remainder of session.

hypotheses.description: LSTM is inactive in ALL training pipelines (V8, V9). Training logs show "18 all-neutral LSTM columns dropped" and no lstm_*.pkl files are saved. The LSTM training loop in train_pipeline_v9.py likely has a sequence-building bug or a min_samples guard that excludes all types. Fix LSTM sequence generation in training pipeline — when active, per-type LSTM AUC in V6 was 0.65-0.70 which improved meta. V9 OOS AUC expected to recover from 0.621 to 0.70+ with LSTM contributing.

New champion (exp_20260310_b1b69c, threshold_long=0.65) produced 0 RTH equity trades on its first live day (Mar 11). Prior champion passed signals at 0.425 global. Hypothesis: threshold 0.65 is over-filtering — backtest at 0.55 and 0.60 to find quality/volume tradeoff. Track next 3 trading days for signal count.
hypotheses.hypothesis: threshold_long=0.65 is suppressing all RTH signals. Optimal threshold may be 0.58-0.62 for RTH equity (balance quality and throughput).

hypotheses.description: All 14 live labels are Type 1 (ORB). No Ph3 VWAP_RECLAIM, Ph2 Pullback, Ph5 PowerHour, or metals/energy VWAP_TREND signals are generating live labels. If non-ORB phases are never trading, their signal types cannot be trained or validated. Hypothesis: Ph3/Ph2/Ph5 entry gates are systematically too restrictive in the current market regime.
hypotheses.hypothesis: Non-ORB phases (Ph2, Ph3, Ph5) have been gated out completely. Relax Ph3 VWAP_RECLAIM ADX threshold from 18 to 15 and Ph2 RSI alignment to see if any signals fire.

hypotheses.description: MCLJ6 London ORB Short on Mar 11 hit TIME_EXIT with -0.805R loss (entry 80.22, exit 85.33, +.11 move). SL was not triggered — price moved well beyond expected range. Mar 10 also had MCLJ6 SL order rejection (price outside allowed range). Energy contracts may need wider SL buffers or a hard position limit check before entry when oil is in a trending regime.
hypotheses.hypothesis: MCLJ6 SL placement needs a minimum price buffer (e.g., 0.5% of price, not just ATR-based) to avoid SL order rejections and ensure exits are triggered before TIME_EXIT.

hypotheses.description: RTH had 0 equity trades today despite 4 instruments active. ML gate at 0.75 may be over-filtering. Evaluate lowering per-type threshold for ORB signal types to 0.65 to restore trade flow without sacrificing edge.
hypotheses.evidence: RTH trades=0 today (2026-03-12), 0 equity entries across MNQH6/MESH6/MYMH6/M2KH6

hypotheses.description: Short ORB signals outperform Long significantly (WR=50% vs 20% live). Apply asymmetric thresholds: lower threshold for Short entries (0.60), maintain or raise for Long entries (0.80).
hypotheses.evidence: Live labels 15 total: Short WR=50% n=2, Long WR=20% n=5, overall Type1 WR=26.7%

hypotheses.description: LONDON_ORB MCLJ6 fired successfully both nights this week (Mar 11 loss, Mar 12 win at same entry 80.22). Two consecutive entries at identical price suggest a structural range setup. Investigate whether MCLJ6 overnight entries cluster at key levels and add range-level confirmation gate.
hypotheses.evidence: Mar 11: MCLJ6 short 80.22 → -27 | Mar 12: MCLJ6 short 80.22 → +94 | Same entry price both nights

hypotheses.description: Live label retraining collapsed V8 AUC to 0.6336 (from V7 0.9880) with only 15 labels (all Type1). Gate live retraining behind: minimum 50 labels per type before including in training, or weight live samples at 0.1x vs historical. Run ablation to confirm.
hypotheses.evidence: V8 AUC=0.6336 with 15 live labels (all Type1 ORB). V7 AUC=0.9880 without live labels. Live WR=26.7% poisoning model.

hypotheses.description: ml_prob=0.5 exactly on ALL 6 trades today — ML gate appears stuck/non-functional. All signals pass at 0.5 regardless of type. Investigate _MLGateV6 inference path.
hypotheses.evidence: All 6 trades: ml_prob=0.5. hf_prob varies (0.77–0.99) but ml_prob always 0.5. Gate is not discriminating.

hypotheses.description: All 5 RTH losses today occurred in first 30 minutes (09:55–10:02 ET). Add hard blackout for 09:30–10:05 ET — skip ALL entries in this window.
hypotheses.evidence: 5/5 RTH trades in 09:55–10:02 ET → all losses, -$77.56. 7-day first_hour WR=35% (n=17). Combined: clear negative edge.

hypotheses.description: Signal type 2 live WR=0% (3/3 losses today, all VWAP_thesis_fail or SL). Mask T2 until live sample grows and WR improves above 40%.
hypotheses.evidence: T2: n=3, WR=0%, all losses in first 30 min RTH. PF likely <0.5.

hypotheses.description: rsi_aligned=False on all 5 RTH trades today — yet trades still fired. RSI alignment gate is soft (advisory) not hard. Make rsi_aligned a hard gate: reject if False.
hypotheses.evidence: All 5 RTH trades: rsi_aligned=False. All lost. Gate allowed entry anyway.

hypotheses.description: Conviction score 0.6–0.7 (high) → all losses today. Conviction=0.3 (low) → yesterday's only win (+$394). Invert or disable conviction sizing multiplier pending recalibration.
hypotheses.evidence: High conv (0.6–0.7): 5 trades, 0 wins. Low conv (0.3): MCLJ6 yesterday +$394. Pattern consistent across 19 live labels.

research_reports.name: Cnn Chart Research
research_reports.content:
# CNN Chart Pattern Research Report

*Research conducted: 2026-02-25*
*Task: Evaluate viability of CNN/vision-based chart pattern recognition for 1-min futures trading (ES/NQ) versus current XGBoost baseline (55.8% WR)*

---

## Executive Summary

- **Evidence is real but domain-specific**: CNN on chart images is a legitimate approach with 10+ peer-reviewed papers. However, virtually all evidence is on **daily equity data**, not intraday futures. The gap between "works on daily DJIA stocks" and "works on 1-min ES futures" is large and undocumented.
- **GAF/MTF encoding shows the most promise**: Gramian Angular Fields outperform raw candlestick images in most comparative studies. They preserve temporal ordering and work well with standard CNNs. However, the best-documented results are still on daily data.
- **Accuracy numbers in papers are misleading**: Many "90%+ accuracy" claims are on synthetic pattern classification (does this image *contain* a head-and-shoulders?), not directional prediction under live trading conditions. Real directional accuracy for next-period returns hovers around 52-58% in credible studies.
- **Transfer learning from ImageNet generally helps**: Pretrained CNN features (ResNet-50, EfficientNet) are reusable after fine-tuning. The improvement is documented. Custom CNNs with 3-5 layers also perform comparably with sufficient data.
- **Verdict: MODERATE evidence. Worth a targeted pilot, not a primary model.** Use as a gate/filter on top of XGBoost, not a standalone signal. Expect 1-2% WR improvement if implemented correctly, with meaningful overfitting risk on short intraday windows.

---

## Paper Reviews

### Paper 1: Sezer & Ozbayoglu (2018) — CNN-TA [Foundational]

**Title**: "Algorithmic Financial Trading with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks: Time Series to Image Conversion Approach"
**Authors**: Omer Berat Sezer, Ahmet Murat Ozbayoglu
**Year**: 2018
**Published**: Applied Soft Computing (Elsevier)
**Citations**: 563+ (as of 2026)
**Link**: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.asoc.2018.04.024

**Instruments**: DJIA 30 stocks
**Timeframe**: Daily
**Method**:
- Converts 15 technical indicators (MACD, RSI, etc.) + 5 price values (OHLCV) into a **20×20 pixel 2D image**
- Not a raw candlestick image — it's a heatmap of technical analysis outputs
- Custom CNN architecture (shallow, ~3 conv layers)
- Labeled: BUY (1), SELL (-1), HOLD (0)

**Key Results**:
- Outperforms Buy-and-Hold strategy
- Outperforms MACD-based and DJI benchmark strategies
- Exact WR not reported; performance measured via cumulative return over 2-5 year backtest periods
- Worked on 9 of 10 DJIA stocks in out-of-sample period

**Limitations**:
- Daily timeframe only
- Technical indicators computed on past data, but normalization uses local window — potential look-ahead bias concern
- No transaction costs in many reported results
- "Outperforms BH" is a low bar in trending markets

**Assessment**: This is the foundational paper that spawned the field. The CNN-TA model is widely cited as a baseline. Critically, the "image" here is NOT a candlestick chart — it's a grid of indicator values. Important distinction for our use case.

---

### Paper 2: Sezer & Ozbayoglu (2019) — CNN-BI [Most Relevant Visual Approach]

**Title**: "Financial Trading Model with Stock Bar Chart Image Time Series with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks"
**Authors**: Omer Berat Sezer, Ahmet Murat Ozbayoglu
**Year**: 2019
**arXiv**: https://arxiv.org/abs/1903.04610
**Citations**: 93

**Instruments**: Dow 30 stocks
**Timeframe**: Daily
**Method**:
- Generates **30-day sliding window bar chart images** from raw OHLCV data
- Actual visual bar chart (not indicator heatmap)
- CNN trained on 2D bar chart images
- Tests on 2007-2012 and 2012-2017 periods (different market regimes)

**Key Results**:
- Outperforms Buy-and-Hold, *especially in trendless or bear markets*
- Underperforms in strong bull markets (the model trades both directions)
- One of first attempts using raw visual chart images as CNN input

**Limitations**:
- Still daily only
- No comparison against LSTM or XGBoost
- Bar chart rendering details not fully specified (color scheme, image resolution unclear)
- "Promising but preliminary" — authors' own characterization

**Assessment**: This is the closest paper to "raw candlestick CNN" for trading. Results are positive but modest. The author explicitly describes it as a preliminary study. Suggests integration as part of an ensemble.

---

### Paper 3: Brim & Flann (2022) — CNN-DDQN with Candlestick Images

**Title**: "Deep Reinforcement Learning Stock Market Trading, Utilizing a CNN with Candlestick Images"
**Authors**: Andrew Brim, Nicholas S. Flann
**Year**: 2022
**Published**: PLoS ONE
**Link**: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0263181
**Citations**: 33

**Instruments**: Top 30 S&P 500 stocks
**Timeframe**: Daily
**Method**:
- CNN embedded within a **Double Deep Q-Network (DDQN)** for reinforcement learning
- Input: **28-candlestick image** (previous 28 days of OHLCV rendered as candlestick chart)
- Actions: long, short, or no position
- Training: 2013-2019 (52,920 observations for 30 stocks)
- Testing: Jan 2020 – Jun 2020 (3,780 observations)

**Key Results**:
- DDQN outperforms S&P 500 Index returns during COVID crash period (Jan-Jun 2020)
- Feature map visualization shows CNN focuses on recent candles during crisis events
- Demonstrated interpretability: CNN attention shifted from all candles to recent candles during the market crash

**Limitations**:
- Test period (6 months of COVID crash) is a cherry-picked regime where shorting capability helps enormously
- Daily equities, not intraday futures
- No transaction cost model specified
- 3,780 test observations is small (30 stocks × ~126 trading days)
- Short sell implementation in practice would face borrowing costs

**Assessment**: Useful demonstration that CNN+RL can work on visual candlestick input. The test period selection during COVID creates survivorship/selection bias. The feature map visualization is genuinely interesting. Not directly applicable to 1-min futures.

---

### Paper 4: Jin & Kwon (2021) — Image Characteristics Study [Most Useful for Implementation]

**Title**: "Impact of Chart Image Characteristics on Stock Price Prediction with a Convolutional Neural Network"
**Authors**: Guangxun Jin, Ohbyung Kwon
**Year**: 2021
**Published**: PLoS ONE
**Link**: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0253121
**Citations**: 19

**Instruments**: Korean Stock Exchange
**Timeframe**: Daily
**Method**:
- Systematic study of **which image rendering choices affect CNN prediction accuracy**
- Variables studied: line style (solid vs dashed), color (green/red vs grayscale), axis marks (present vs absent), multiple chart elements

**Key Results** (most actionable findings in entire literature):
1. **Solid lines > dashed lines** for price rendering
2. **Color (green/red candlesticks) > grayscale** — color encodes directional information the CNN can use
3. **No axis marks = better accuracy** — axis text is visual noise for CNN
4. **Single image without decorations outperforms composite images**
5. Stock price predictions can be made with images alone (no numerical data needed)

**Limitations**:
- Daily Korean stocks; regime may differ from US intraday futures
- Accuracy numbers not reported in abstract (study focuses on relative comparisons between image types)
- Did not test GAF vs candlestick head-to-head

**Assessment**: This is the most practically useful paper for implementation decisions. Key takeaways: use color candlesticks, remove axis labels and tick marks, use solid rendering, keep images clean. These are zero-cost implementation choices.

---

### Paper 5: Jiang & Liang (2017) — Deep RL Portfolio Management [Foundational]

**Title**: "A Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for the Financial Portfolio Management Problem"
**Authors**: Zhengyao Jiang, Dixing Xu, Jinjun Liang
**Year**: 2017
**arXiv**: https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.10059
**Citations**: 1,200+

**Instruments**: Cryptocurrency (Bitcoin and altcoins)
**Timeframe**: 30-minute trading periods
**Method**:
- EIIE (Ensemble of Identical Independent Evaluators) topology
- Three versions: CNN, RNN, LSTM on price history
- CNN uses 2D price history matrix (not rendered chart image) — rows=assets, cols=time steps
- Portfolio-Vector Memory (PVM) for position tracking

**Key Results**:
- All three versions outperform other portfolio strategies
- CNN version achieves 4-fold+ returns in 50 days (cryptocurrency bull period)
- Framework beats equal-weight, momentum, and mean-variance portfolios

**Limitations**:
- Cryptocurrency is not the same market as regulated futures
- 2017 crypto bull run — survivorship bias extreme
- 50-day backtest window is extremely short
- 0.25% commission per trade is low for crypto; execution costs in futures differ significantly
- Portfolio approach ≠ directional prediction (different problem formulation)

**Assessment**: Influential paper but poor analogy for our use case. The CNN here reads a matrix of price ratios, not a rendered chart image. Crypto in 2017 had extreme returns; results wouldn't replicate in efficient futures markets.

---

### Paper 6: Tsai et al. (2022) — Dynamic Deep Convolutional Candlestick Learner (GAF + YOLO)

**Title**: "Dynamic Deep Convolutional Candlestick Learner"
**Authors**: Yun-Cheng Tsai et al.
**Year**: 2022
**arXiv**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.08669

**Instruments**: Not specified (technical analysis focus)
**Timeframe**: Not specified
**Method**:
- Integrates **GAF (Gramian Angular Field) encoding** with **YOLO v1 object detection**
- Uses GAF to encode candlestick time series as 2D images
- Object detection for candlestick pattern *location* (not just classification)
- Detects multiple pattern types in one image

**Key Results**:
- GAF-CNN model "well-suited to imitate how human traders capture candlestick patterns"
- Object detection approach enables real-time pattern detection
- Results described as "tremendous potential" — qualitative, not quantitative

**Limitations**:
- No trading backtest results reported
- No comparison against other approaches
- Pattern classification ≠ profitable trading

**Assessment**: Technically interesting but no financial evidence. GAF+YOLO is a clever idea for pattern detection, but "classifying patterns" and "making money" are different problems. Skip until backtested.

---

### Paper 7: Cohen, Balch & Veloso (2020) — Trading via Image Classification [Industry]

**Title**: "Trading via Image Classification"
**Authors**: Naftali Cohen, Tucker Balch, Manuela Veloso (Morgan Stanley AI)
**Year**: 2020
**Published**: ACM International Conference on AI in Finance
**Link**: https://dl.acm.org/doi/abs/10.1145/3383455.3422544

**Instruments**: Not specified (financial time series)
**Timeframe**: Not specified
**Method**:
- Creates large sample of financial time series images as **candlestick (Box and Whisker) charts**
- Labels samples using three **algebraically-defined binary trade strategies** (e.g., Murphy technical rules)
- Trains 12+ ML classifiers on images (CNNs, SVMs, random forests)

**Key Results**:
- "Algorithms efficiently recover the complicated, multiscale label-generating rules when the data is visually represented"
- CNN outperforms other classifiers at recovering visual signals
- Transformation from time-series to vision problem is "useful for recovering signals typical of technical analysis"

**Limitations**:
- Key limitation: labels are **algebraically derived** (the "truth" is a formulaic strategy, not actual market alpha)
- Demonstrating that CNNs can re-learn a mathematical formula is not the same as discovering new alpha
- No live trading results; no actual forward P&L

**Assessment**: This paper proves that CNNs can visually learn what technical rules look like. It does NOT prove those technical rules are profitable. Important conceptual distinction. The Morgan Stanley authorship adds credibility to the methodology.

---

### Paper 8: Wang & Oates (2015) — Gramian Angular Fields [Foundational GAF Paper]

**Title**: "Imaging Time-Series to Improve Classification and Imputation"
**Authors**: Zhiguang Wang, Tim Oates
**Year**: 2015
**Published**: IJCAI 2015

**Method**: GAF Computation:
1. Normalize time series to [-1, 1]: x̃_i = (x_i - max(x) + x_i - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x))
2. Convert to polar coordinates: φ_i = arccos(x̃_i), r_i = t_i / N
3. **GASF** (Gramian Angular Summation Field): G[i,j] = cos(φ_i + φ_j)
4. **GADF** (Gramian Angular Difference Field): G[i,j] = sin(φ_i − φ_j)
5. **MTF** (Markov Transition Field): Discretize series into Q bins → transition probability matrix M → MTF[i,j] = p(q_j|q_i) for temporal positions i,j

**Key Results**:
- GAF encoding + CNN outperforms raw time series + CNN on UCR time series classification benchmark
- MTF adds complementary information (transition dynamics)
- Combining GASF + GADF + MTF (3-channel image) often outperforms individual channels

**Limitations**:
- Original paper uses UCR benchmark datasets (physiological, sensor data) — NOT financial data
- Financial time series have different statistical properties (fat tails, non-stationarity, regime changes)

**Assessment**: This is the origin paper. GAF computation is straightforward and deterministic. The improvement over raw time series in classification is real — but generalization to financial prediction requires validation.

---

### Paper 9: Ghasemieh & Kashef (2023) — GAF-EWGAN for Stock Prediction

**Title**: "An Enhanced Wasserstein GAN with Gramian Angular Fields for Efficient Stock Market Prediction During Market Crash Periods"
**Authors**: A. Ghasemieh, R. Kashef
**Year**: 2023
**Published**: Applied Intelligence (Springer)
**Citations**: 19

**Instruments**: Stock market indices (multiple)
**Timeframe**: Daily
**Method**:
- GAF encoding of time series
- EWGAN (Enhanced Wasserstein GAN) for data augmentation
- Stacking ensemble model
- Focus on crash periods specifically

**Key Results**:
- Improved prediction accuracy during market crash periods vs. non-GAF baselines
- EWGAN augmentation helps during low-data crisis periods

**Limitations**:
- Daily stocks only
- Crisis-period focus creates selection bias
- No trading P&L reported

**Assessment**: Shows GAF helps during regime changes. Relevant if our bot needs to handle volatile intraday sessions.

---

### Paper 10: Sarıkoç & Çelik (2025) — GAF-AlexNet for ETF Prediction

**Title**: "Convolutional Neural Network Analysis of the Gramian Angular Field for Oil Services Exchange Traded Fund (ETF) Prediction"
**Year**: 2025
**Published**: International Journal of Management Information Systems

**Key Results**:
- GAF-AlexNet model achieved **55% accuracy** on ETF directional prediction

**Assessment**: **55% is barely above chance (50/50 random baseline).** This is actually honest reporting and suggests the edge is thin even with GAF. This is the most realistic expectation: ~55% directional accuracy, not 90%.

---

### Paper 11: Ghoshal & Roberts (2020) — Thresholded ConvNet Ensembles [Important Negative Result]

**Title**: "Thresholded ConvNet Ensembles: Neural Networks for Technical Forecasting"
**Authors**: S. Ghoshal, S. Roberts
**Year**: 2020
**Published**: Neural Computing and Applications (Springer)
**Citations**: 19

**Instruments**: S&P 500 Index
**Timeframe**: Daily
**Method**: CNN ensemble on multi-day price patterns

**Key Results** (NEGATIVE):
- "16 multi-day patterns produces **no significant alteration**" in accuracy
- CNN ensemble yields only "modest gains" over baselines
- Technical chart patterns may not contain significant predictive information

**Assessment**: Important counterevidence. Even with careful implementation, multi-day chart patterns had no significant effect on S&P 500 prediction. This is a controlled study with honest null results.

---

### Paper 12: Zhang et al. (2023) — Interpretable Image-Based Deep Learning for ETFs

**Title**: "Interpretable Image-Based Deep Learning for Price Trend Prediction in ETF Markets"
**Authors**: R. Zhang, C. Zhao, G. Lin
**Year**: 2023
**Published**: European Journal of Finance
**Citations**: 10

**Key Methodology Notes**:
- Data split **chronologically** to avoid look-ahead bias
- Explicitly mitigates overfitting risk in low signal-to-noise environment
- Different from CNN-TA (image here is actual chart, not indicator heatmap)

**Assessment**: Good methodological example. The emphasis on chronological splits and overfitting mitigation is the right approach. Confirms the problem is hard.

---

### Paper 13: Vosmanský (2022) — Czech Thesis, Highest Reported Accuracy [CAUTION]

**Title**: "Price Gaps in the Stock Market"
**Year**: 2022
**Published**: Master's Thesis, Charles University Prague
**Link**: https://dspace.cuni.cz

**Key Result**: "Feeding candlestick chart into CNN reaches **74.2% accuracy**"

**Assessment**: ⚠️ CAUTION. 74.2% accuracy on stock direction prediction from candlestick charts alone would be extraordinary alpha. This is almost certainly on training data or a regime-specific test set. No independent replication. Treat as unreliable.

---

### Paper 14: Lin et al. (2026) — Combining Line, Candlestick, and Bar Charts

**Title**: "Image-Based Deep Learning Models for Stock Predictions: Combining Line, Candlestick, and Bar Charts"
**Authors**: WC Lin, MC Wang, CF Tsai, JP Hsu
**Year**: 2026
**Published**: Journal of Forecasting (Wiley)

**Method**:
- Compares line chart, candlestick, and bar chart encodings
- 20 trading day lookback window
- CNN, LSTM, and hybrid CNN-LSTM
- Random guessing baseline: 50/50

**Assessment**: Most recent paper. 20-day window is the emerging consensus for daily charts.

---

## Open Source Projects

### Project 1: Sezer et al. GitHub Implementations
- Multiple community re-implementations of CNN-TA exist on GitHub (search "CNN-TA sezer stock")
- Original code not publicly released by authors
- Quality varies significantly

### Project 2: Parmar (2024) — CNN-DDQN with GAF
- **URL**: https://norma.ncirl.ie/id/eprint/7246
- 24-day GAF images as input to CNN-DDQN
- Reports 90.7% accuracy on real-world data, 92.4% on synthetic
- ⚠️ **This is classification accuracy on labeled pattern images, not live trading P&L.** Very different metric.
- Code and dataset not publicly available

### Project 3: pyts Library (GAF/MTF Implementation)
- **URL**: https://github.com/johannfaouzi/pyts
- Production-ready Python library for time series imaging
- Implements GASF, GADF, MTF, RP (Recurrence Plots)
- Integrates with scikit-learn
- **Actively maintained**; best starting point for GAF implementation

### Project 4: tslearn Library
- **URL**: https://github.com/tslearn-team/tslearn
- Also implements time series imaging
- Broader scope than pyts

### Project 5: Cohen, Balch, Veloso (2020) — No public code
- Morgan Stanley paper; code not released
- Methodology is well-described; reproducible from paper

### Key Observation on GitHub
No found GitHub repository demonstrates **profitable live trading** from CNN chart images on futures. Community implementations exist for stocks/crypto on daily data. The gap between "fun academic project" and "live trading edge" is enormous.

---

## Key Findings

### Finding 1: Evidence is Real but Constrained to Daily Equity Markets
All credible papers with positive results test on **daily stocks or indices**. Signal/noise ratio on daily data is ~10-50x better than 1-minute intraday. The field has not produced reliable evidence for intraday futures.

### Finding 2: Realistic Directional Accuracy = 52-58%
- Honest studies report 52-58% directional accuracy
- "74.2%" and "90.7%" figures are likely data leakage or classification tasks
- **55% WR from GAF-AlexNet (Sarıkoç 2025)** is probably the most honest comparable
- Our current XGBoost WR of 55.8% is already at or above what this approach achieves in daily studies

### Finding 3: Color > Grayscale (Confirmed)
Jin & Kwon (2021) confirm: green/red color encoding outperforms grayscale. For candlestick charts, color is not decoration — it encodes bullish/bearish direction.

### Finding 4: GAF/MTF Generally Outperforms Raw Candlestick Images
Multiple papers show GAF outperforms raw pixel rendering for classification tasks. Reasons:
- GAF preserves temporal relationships in the correlation matrix
- Invariant to scale (normalized to [-1,1])
- Works with standard CNNs without needing to "see" candlestick shapes
- MTF captures transition dynamics (momentum information)

### Finding 5: Lookback Window Consensus = 20-30 Bars (for daily)
- CNN-BI: 30 bars
- Brim & Flann: 28 bars
- Lin et al.: 20 bars
- For **1-minute intraday**, the analogous window would be 20-30 minutes of the session — very local context. Consider using 5-min bars with 20-30 bar lookback instead.

### Finding 6: Transfer Learning Generally Helps, But Is Not Magic
- ImageNet pretrained features (edge detection, texture) transfer to chart images
- Fine-tuning required (financial charts ≠ natural images)
- EfficientNet-B0/B1 is the current sweet spot (small enough to train on limited data, capable enough to extract features)
- Custom shallow CNNs can match pretrained models with >50K training samples

### Finding 7: Data Augmentation Is Risky for Financial Charts
- Time stretching: **VALID** (different session lengths preserve pattern shape)
- Vertical flipping: **INVALID** (inverts bullish/bearish meaning)
- Adding small Gaussian noise: **VALID if small** (<1% of bar range)
- Horizontal flipping: **ABSOLUTELY INVALID** (reverses time)
- Random rotation: **INVALID** (distorts bar proportions)

### Finding 8: The Critical Failure Mode Is Overfitting + Data Leakage
Most common reasons papers fail in real deployment:
1. **Look-ahead bias**: Using normalization that incorporates future data (e.g., normalizing bar heights using the future max of the test period)
2. **Non-chronological splits**: Randomly shuffling time series data (trains on future, tests on past)
3. **Ignoring transaction costs**: Many papers don't include slippage or commissions
4. **Short test windows**: Test on 6 months after training on 7 years = 1:14 ratio (overfitting paradise)
5. **Label construction**: How you define "profit" in the label matters enormously

---

## Recommended Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Quick Feasibility Test (2-3 weeks)

**Encoding**: Use GAF (Gramian Angular Summation Field) — not raw candlestick pixels
- Reason: More mathematically principled, invariant to scale, compatible with standard CNN input
- Library: `pyts` (production-ready implementation)

**Timeframe**: Use **5-minute bars aggregated to 30-bar windows** (not raw 1-min)
- 1-min bars: signal/noise too low
- 30 bars × 5 min = 150 minutes of context (2.5 hours of session data)
- This captures morning session context and aligns with human chart analysis timeframes

**Image Size**: Start with **64×64 pixels** 
- 32×32: Loses detail, especially for longer windows
- 128×128: Requires ~4x more data to train effectively
- 64×64 is the sweet spot in the literature

**CNN Architecture**: EfficientNet-B0 with ImageNet pretrained weights
- ~5.3M parameters (small enough for limited data)
- Fine-tune final 2 layers; freeze early layers initially
- Binary output: next-period up/down (or long/flat if you don't short)

**Lookback**: 30 bars (5-min bars = 2.5 hours of data per image)

**Label**: 1 if close[t+3] > close[t] + 0.5×ATR[t], else 0
- Use ATR-normalized labeling (not fixed tick threshold)
- 3-bar forward window matches your trade holding period

### Phase 2: Honest Evaluation (4-6 weeks)

**Train/Test Split**:
- Training: First 18 months of data (chronological)
- Validation: Next 3 months
- Test: Final 3 months (never touched during training)
- Walk-forward validation with quarterly retraining

**Baseline Comparisons**:
- XGBoost current baseline (55.8% WR)
- Random classifier (50% WR)
- Buy-and-hold equivalent

**Success Criteria**:
- WR > 57% on test set (meaningful lift over XGBoost)
- Precision-recall curve area > 0.60
- Must survive transaction cost simulation (commissions + slippage)

### Phase 3: Integration Strategy

**Use as GATE, not standalone signal**:
```python
# Example integration
def entry_signal(xgboost_signal, cnn_probability):
    if xgboost_signal == LONG and cnn_probability > 0.60:
        return LONG  # Both agree → high confidence entry
    elif xgboost_signal == LONG and cnn_probability < 0.40:
        return SKIP  # CNN disagrees → skip trade
    else:
        return xgboost_signal  # CNN neutral → follow XGBoost
```

**Rationale**: CNN and XGBoost capture different types of information (visual patterns vs. feature-engineered statistics). Agreement between them provides a natural filter.

---

## Red Flags / Reasons to Skip

### Red Flag 1: No Evidence on 1-Minute Intraday Futures Specifically
Every paper with positive results uses daily stocks or at minimum 5-30 minute crypto. The 1-minute ES/NQ signal environment is one of the noisiest in all of finance. What works at daily granularity may not work at 1-minute.

### Red Flag 2: Honest GAF Accuracy = 55% (Same as Your Baseline)
The most honest recent result (Sarıkoç 2025, GAF-AlexNet on ETFs) achieved 55% directional accuracy. Your XGBoost already achieves 55.8% WR. There may be no net improvement.

### Red Flag 3: Ghoshal & Roberts (2020) — Null Result on Patterns
A controlled study found that increasing from 1 to 16 multi-day chart patterns produced "no significant alteration" in S&P 500 returns. This is evidence that visual pattern information may already be priced in.

### Red Flag 4: Data Requirements Are High
- EfficientNet-B0 benefits from 50,000+ training samples minimum
- At 30-bar / 5-min windows per image: need ~50,000 non-overlapping windows
- At 5-min bars with 6.5-hour sessions: ~78 bars/day → ~0.5 non-overlapping windows/day
- Need ~100,000 trading days to get 50,000 samples with non-overlapping windows
- **This is impossible for a single instrument**
- Mitigation: Use overlapping windows (stride=1) — but this inflates apparent dataset size while creating serial correlation

### Red Flag 5: Overfitting Is Especially Likely Here
- CNNs have millions of parameters
- Financial time series have very low signal-to-noise ratio
- Short history (ES futures available since 1997 electronically, NQ since 1999)
- At 1-min bars: ~1.3M bars of ES available — sounds like a lot, but converting to non-overlapping 30-bar windows = only ~43,000 samples over 25 years

### Red Flag 6: Candlestick Colors for Futures May Not Help
Jin & Kwon (2021) showed color helps for stocks. For futures, the color of an individual 1-minute bar contains almost no predictive information — the market moves too fast and is mean-reverting at short scales.

---

## Data Requirements for Our Use Case

### Minimum Requirements
- **Instrument**: ES or NQ 1-minute OHLCV data
- **History needed**: Minimum 5 years (2019-2024) of clean 1-min data
- **Bars available**: ~390 bars/day × 252 trading days × 5 years ≈ 491,400 1-min bars
- **At 5-min aggregation**: ~98,280 5-min bars
- **Sliding windows (30 bars, stride=1)**: ~98,250 samples
- **Non-overlapping windows**: ~3,276 samples ← **dangerously small**

### Recommended Approach to Increase Dataset Size
1. **Multi-instrument**: Train on ES + NQ + YM + RTY simultaneously (4 instruments × 5 years = ~400K total samples with stride-1 windows)
2. **5-minute bars**: Aggregate 1-min to 5-min → larger meaningful windows per session
3. **Overlapping windows with caution**: Use stride=5 (not stride=1) to reduce serial correlation while retaining data
4. **No augmentation**: Stick to real data; GAF encoding already provides regularization

### CNN Training Resources Needed
- GPU training recommended: Training 50K images on EfficientNet-B0: ~2-4 hours on RTX 3090
- RAM: 16GB minimum (images in memory during training)
- Storage: 50K × 64×64×3 images ≈ 600MB (manageable)

### Labeling Strategy
```python
# Example labeling for futures
def label_sample(bars, t, forward_bars=3, atr_multiplier=0.5):
    """
    Returns 1 (long signal) if price rises by >0.5 ATR in next 3 bars.
    Returns 0 (no signal) if price falls or moves less than threshold.
    """
    atr = compute_atr(bars[t-14:t])  # 14-period ATR
    future_return = bars['close'][t + forward_bars] - bars['close'][t]
    threshold = atr_multiplier * atr
    return 1 if future_return > threshold else 0
```

---

## Summary Judgment

| Dimension | Assessment |
|-----------|------------|
| Evidence quality | MODERATE (real papers, real results, but different domain) |
| Applicability to 1-min futures | LOW (no direct evidence) |
| Expected WR improvement | +0 to +2% over baseline (optimistic: +3%) |
| Data requirement feasibility | CHALLENGING (need multi-instrument approach) |
| Overfitting risk | HIGH |
| Implementation complexity | MEDIUM-HIGH (4-6 weeks of engineering) |
| Recommended priority | SECONDARY / EXPLORATORY |

**Bottom line**: The approach is not pseudoscience — it has real academic backing. But the honest expected lift over your current 55.8% XGBoost baseline is small (probably 0-2%), and the failure modes are serious. Start with a 2-week feasibility test using GAF + EfficientNet-B0 on 5-minute bars before committing significant engineering time.

---

*Research sources:*
- *Sezer & Ozbayoglu (2018, 2019), Applied Soft Computing / arXiv:1903.04610*
- *Brim & Flann (2022), PLoS ONE doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0263181*
- *Jin & Kwon (2021), PLoS ONE doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0253121*
- *Jiang & Liang (2017), arXiv:1706.10059*
- *Tsai et al. (2022), arXiv:2201.08669*
- *Cohen, Balch & Veloso (2020), ACM AI in Finance*
- *Wang & Oates (2015), IJCAI*
- *Ghasemieh & Kashef (2023), Applied Intelligence*
- *Sarıkoç & Çelik (2025), IJMIS*
- *Ghoshal & Roberts (2020), Neural Computing and Applications*
- *Zhang et al. (2023), European Journal of Finance*
- *Lin et al. (2026), Journal of Forecasting*
- *Vosmanský (2022), Charles University Prague thesis*
- *Haggett (2025), dustinhaggett.com/papers/chart-vision-paper.pdf*

research_reports.name: Overnight Strategy Report
research_reports.content:
# Overnight Strategy Research Report
**Generated:** 2026-02-23  
**Backtest Period:** Nov 2025 – Feb 2026 (90 days, 1-hour bars)  
**Data Proxies:** GC=F, SI=F, HG=F, CL=F, NG=F via yfinance  
**Strategies Tested:** London Open Range Breakout, Asian Session Continuation, Pre-Market VWAP Reclaim, EMA Momentum  

---

## Executive Summary

Three instruments show statistically meaningful overnight edges. Two (Silver, Copper) do **not** — avoid trading those overnight until more data supports an edge.

| Instrument | Best Strategy | Win Rate | Profit Factor | Net (90d) | Recommended Hours (ET) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **MGCJ6** (Gold)      | Asian Continuation   | 55.6% | 1.52 | +$1,156 | 00:00–06:00 |
| **MCLJ6** (Crude Oil) | London ORB           | 53.8% | 1.42 | +$286  | 03:00–05:30 |
| **MNGJ6** (Nat Gas)   | Asian Contin. + EMA  | 39.4% | 2.49 | +$725  | 00:00–06:00 |
| **SILH6** (Silver)    | ❌ No edge found     | 25.0% | 0.45 | –$810  | — |
| **MHGH6** (Copper)    | ❌ No edge found     | 39.2% | 0.74 | –$670  | — |

---

## 1. Gold (MGCJ6) — Asian Session Continuation

### How It Works
Gold is heavily driven by the Asian session (Tokyo + Shanghai). When gold trends during the 8:00 PM – 12:00 AM ET window, that momentum frequently continues into the London open. The strategy enters at midnight ET in the direction of the Asian session trend, targeting a move of 0.8× the Asian session range with a stop of 0.5× the range.

### Parameters
- **Asian session:** 20:00–23:59 ET (previous day)
- **Entry window:** 00:00–05:59 ET
- **Stop loss:** 0.5× Asian session range
- **Take profit:** 0.8× Asian session range  
- **R:R implied:** 1.6:1 (but wins slightly more often → edge)
- **Min range filter:** Asian session must move > 0.2% to be tradeable

### Backtest Results (90 days)
- **Trades:** 36
- **Win Rate:** 55.6%
- **Profit Factor:** 1.52
- **Avg Win:** $169.48 | **Avg Loss:** -$139.59
- **Net P&L:** +$1,156
- **Max Drawdown:** -$766

### Entry/Exit Rules
1. At 20:00 ET, record Asian session open price
2. At 23:59 ET, record Asian session close, high, and low
3. Compute trend: if close > open → bullish; close < open → bearish
4. Filter: skip if session range < 0.2% of price
5. At 00:00 ET: enter long (bullish) or short (bearish) on open of first 1-min bar
6. SL = 0.5 × session range below (long) / above (short) entry
7. TP = 0.8 × session range above (long) / below (short) entry
8. Hard exit at 06:00 ET regardless (before London peak liquidity event)
9. **Skip on FOMC, CPI, NFP, EIA days** (news filter required)

### Risk Sizing (MGCJ6)
- Tick = $0.10 / $1.00 per tick
- Typical Asian range ~$5–$15 → SL ~$2.50–$7.50 per oz → $25–$75 on Micro Gold (33.2 oz)
- This is **well within $300 risk limit** for 1–3 contracts
- **Recommended:** 1 contract unless range is < $5; then 2 contracts

---

## 2. Crude Oil (MCLJ6) — London Open Range Breakout

### How It Works
WTI crude oil has strong directional moves at the London open (02:00–06:00 ET) driven by European demand signals, OPEC news cycle, and inventory anticipation. The strategy defines a 1-hour range at 02:00–02:59 ET and enters on the first breakout of that range.

### Parameters
- **Range period:** 02:00–02:59 ET
- **Entry window:** 03:00–05:30 ET
- **Stop loss:** 1× range width (placed at opposite end of range)
- **Take profit:** 1.5× range width (tested RR 1.5:1 → 3:1, all positive)
- **Best RR tested:** 1.5:1 (highest win rate); 3.0:1 (highest PF)
- **Recommended:** Start with 1.5:1, adjust upward as confidence grows

### Backtest Results (90 days, RR=1.5:1)
- **Trades:** 52
- **Win Rate:** 53.8%
- **Profit Factor:** 1.42
- **Avg Win:** $34.73 | **Avg Loss:** -$28.62
- **Net P&L:** +$286 (conservative), up to +$534 with 3:1 RR
- **Max Drawdown:** -$121

### Entry/Exit Rules
1. At 02:00 ET: start recording range (high/low of 02:00 candle only)
2. At 03:00 ET: enter on first 1-minute close above range high → long; below range low → short
3. SL = range width below entry (long) / above entry (short)
4. TP = 1.5× range width (initial; can trail to 3:1 if first 1-hour closes in profit)
5. Max 1 trade per day in crude overnight session
6. Hard exit at 09:00 ET (before EIA weekly inventory report window)
7. **Skip Wednesdays near EIA report** (typically 10:30 AM ET but occasionally 11:00 AM ET)

### Risk Sizing (MCLJ6)
- Tick = $0.01 / $1.00 per tick
- Typical London range ~$0.40–$1.20 → SL $40–$120 per contract
- **Well within $300 limit** for 1–2 contracts

---

## 3. Natural Gas (MNGJ6) — Asian Continuation + EMA Filter

### How It Works
Nat gas is highly volatile with strong overnight momentum. The Asian session (20:00–00:00 ET) frequently sets directional bias. Adding an EMA(8)/EMA(21) alignment filter on 1-hour bars dramatically improves selectivity: only take the trade if the EMAs confirm the same direction as the Asian trend. This cuts trades by ~40% but nearly doubles the profit factor.

### Parameters
- **Asian session:** 20:00–23:59 ET (previous day)
- **Entry window:** 00:00–06:00 ET
- **EMA filter:** 1-hour EMA(8) must be above EMA(21) for longs, below for shorts
- **Stop loss:** 0.4× Asian session range
- **Take profit:** 1.5–2.5× Asian session range (best tested: 2.5×, PF=2.64)
- **Recommended start:** TP=1.5×, build confidence before widening

### Backtest Results (90 days, TP=2.5x SL=0.4x)
- **Trades:** 33
- **Win Rate:** 36.4%
- **Profit Factor:** 2.64
- **Avg Win:** $109.50 | **Avg Loss:** -$23.73
- **Net P&L:** +$816
- **Max Drawdown:** -$192

⚠️ **Note:** 36% win rate means 6-7 consecutive losses possible. Requires psychological discipline. The fat avg-win vs tight avg-loss is the edge (asymmetric R:R of ~4.6:1 realized).

### Entry/Exit Rules
1. At 20:00 ET: start tracking Asian session
2. At 23:59 ET: compute Asian range, trend direction
3. At 00:00 ET: check EMA(8) vs EMA(21) on 1-hour chart
4. Only enter if EMA direction **matches** Asian trend direction
5. Enter at open of 00:00 candle
6. SL = 0.4× Asian range
7. TP = 1.5–2.5× Asian range
8. Hard exit at 06:00 ET
9. **Skip on EIA Nat Gas Storage report days** (Thursdays 10:30 AM ET — no overnight the night before if you're unsure of expiry)

### Risk Sizing (MNGJ6)
- Tick = $0.001 / $1.00 per tick
- Nat gas Asian range typically $0.05–$0.20 → SL $50–$200 per contract
- Monitor carefully: NG is volatile; on high-range days, skip or reduce to 1 contract

---

## 4. Silver (SILH6) — NO EDGE FOUND

All four strategies tested negative over 90 days. Silver during overnight hours showed:
- High spread relative to range (noise-dominated)
- Low volume leading to erratic price action
- No consistent session-specific directional bias

**Recommendation:** Disable overnight trading for Silver. Silver can be traded during RTH (09:30–16:00 ET) using the ORB_VWAP strategy alongside gold, but the overnight edge is not statistically reliable.

---

## 5. Copper (MHGH6) — NO EDGE FOUND

All strategies tested between PF=0.58–0.74 (all negative). Copper overnight showed:
- Range-bound behavior with frequent whipsaws at London open
- Asian session trend reversal rather than continuation (mean-reverting)
- Small tick value limits profit potential vs. noise cost

**Recommendation:** Disable overnight trading for Copper. Consider a **mean-reversion** approach (fade the Asian move at 00:00 ET) if you want to pursue copper overnight — this is the opposite of what works for gold/natgas.

---

## Implementation Notes

### News Filter (Critical)
Maintain a blackout calendar. Skip any overnight trade if the following day has:
- **FOMC** (rate decision day + day before)
- **CPI** (Consumer Price Index)
- **EIA Petroleum Inventory** (Wednesdays, skip crude overnight)
- **EIA Nat Gas Storage** (Thursdays, skip NG overnight)
- **NFP** (Non-Farm Payrolls, Friday — impacts all metals)

### Session Hours Summary
```
Gold (MGCJ6):     Entry 00:00–00:05 ET  |  Exit 06:00 ET
Crude (MCLJ6):    Entry 03:00–05:30 ET  |  Exit 09:00 ET  
Nat Gas (MNGJ6):  Entry 00:00–00:05 ET  |  Exit 06:00 ET
```

### Risk Limits Per Trade
| Instrument | Typical SL | Max Contracts | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| MGCJ6 | $50–$100 | 2 | Increase if Asian range < $5 |
| MCLJ6 | $40–$120 | 2 | 1 contract if range > $1 |
| MNGJ6 | $50–$150 | 1 | Highly volatile; strict 1-contract rule |

---

## Python Signal Functions

Drop these into `bot.py`. Each function takes `bars_1m` (list of 1-minute OHLCV dicts from SignalR) and returns `'long'`, `'short'`, or `None`.

```python
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# OVERNIGHT STRATEGY SIGNALS — Drop into bot.py
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
import statistics
from datetime import time as dtime, datetime, timezone
import pytz

ET = pytz.timezone('America/New_York')

def _now_et():
    return datetime.now(ET)

def _bars_between(bars_1m: list, start_h: int, start_m: int,
                  end_h: int, end_m: int, date_str: str = None) -> list:
    """Filter 1-min bars between two ET times on a given date (today if None)."""
    result = []
    for b in bars_1m:
        ts = b['timestamp']
        if isinstance(ts, (int, float)):
            ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts, tz=ET)
        elif ts.tzinfo is None:
            ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=ET)
        else:
            ts = ts.astimezone(ET)
        t = ts.time()
        if dtime(start_h, start_m) <= t < dtime(end_h, end_m):
            result.append(b)
    return result


def _ema(values: list, period: int) -> float:
    """Simple EMA from a list of floats (most recent last)."""
    if len(values) < period:
        return None
    k = 2 / (period + 1)
    ema = values[0]
    for v in values[1:]:
        ema = v * k + ema * (1 - k)
    return ema


def _atr(bars: list, period: int = 14) -> float:
    """ATR from 1-min bars."""
    if len(bars) < 2:
        return None
    trs = []
    for i in range(1, len(bars)):
        high  = bars[i]['high']
        low   = bars[i]['low']
        prev_c = bars[i-1]['close']
        tr = max(high - low, abs(high - prev_c), abs(low - prev_c))
        trs.append(tr)
    if len(trs) < period:
        return statistics.mean(trs)
    return statistics.mean(trs[-period:])


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STRATEGY 1: Gold Asian Session Continuation
# Symbol: MGCJ6
# Hours: Enter 00:00–00:05 ET, Exit 06:00 ET
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class GoldAsianContinuationSignal:
    """
    Tracks the Asian session (20:00–23:59 ET) and fires a signal at midnight.
    Call update(bars_1m) each minute. Call signal() to get current trade signal.
    """
    MIN_RANGE_PCT = 0.002   # 0.2% minimum Asian range filter
    SL_RATIO      = 0.5     # stop = 0.5× Asian range
    TP_RATIO      = 0.8     # target = 0.8× Asian range

    def __init__(self):
        self._state = {}     # keyed by date string

    def _get_asian_stats(self, bars_1m: list, prev_date_str: str) -> dict | None:
        """Extract stats from the previous day's Asian session bars."""
        asian = _bars_between(bars_1m, 20, 0, 23, 59, prev_date_str)
        if len(asian) < 10:
            return None
        open_p  = asian[0]['open']
        close_p = asian[-1]['close']
        high_p  = max(b['high']  for b in asian)
        low_p   = min(b['low']   for b in asian)
        rng     = high_p - low_p
        if rng / close_p < self.MIN_RANGE_PCT:
            return None
        return {
            'open': open_p, 'close': close_p,
            'high': high_p, 'low': low_p, 'range': rng,
            'bullish': close_p > open_p,
        }

    def signal(self, bars_1m: list, news_day: bool = False) -> dict | None:
        """
        Returns signal dict or None.
        Call this function every minute between 00:00–00:05 ET.
        
        Args:
            bars_1m:   all 1-minute bars for current session (enough to cover 20:00 prior)
            news_day:  True if today has FOMC/CPI/NFP — skip trade
            
        Returns:
            {
                'direction': 'long' | 'short',
                'stop_pts': float,   # points (price units)
                'target_pts': float,
                'instrument': 'MGCJ6'
            }
            or None if no trade
        """
        if news_day:
            return None
        
        now = _now_et()
        # Only fire in entry window 00:00–00:05 ET
        if not (dtime(0, 0) <= now.time() <= dtime(0, 5)):
            return None
        
        stats = self._get_asian_stats(bars_1m, prev_date_str=None)
        if stats is None:
            return None
        
        sl_pts = self.SL_RATIO * stats['range']
        tp_pts = self.TP_RATIO * stats['range']
        
        return {
            'direction'  : 'long' if stats['bullish'] else 'short',
            'stop_pts'   : sl_pts,
            'target_pts' : tp_pts,
            'instrument' : 'MGCJ6',
            'exit_time'  : dtime(6, 0),
            'rationale'  : f"Asian range ${stats['range']:.2f}, "
                           f"{'bullish' if stats['bullish'] else 'bearish'} session",
        }


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STRATEGY 2: Crude Oil London Open Range Breakout
# Symbol: MCLJ6
# Hours: Range 02:00–02:59 ET, Entry 03:00–05:30 ET
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class CrudeOilLondonORBSignal:
    """
    London Open Range Breakout for WTI Crude.
    Range defined by 02:00–02:59 ET candles.
    Breakout at 03:00–05:30 ET.
    """
    RR_RATIO = 1.5   # take-profit = 1.5× stop

    def __init__(self):
        self._fired_today = False
        self._last_date   = None

    def signal(self, bars_1m: list, news_day: bool = False) -> dict | None:
        """
        Returns signal dict or None.
        Call every minute between 03:00–05:30 ET.
        
        Args:
            bars_1m:  1-minute bars covering at least 02:00–current ET today
            news_day: skip if EIA Wednesday or other news
            
        Returns:
            signal dict or None
        """
        if news_day:
            return None
        
        now = _now_et()
        today = now.date()
        
        # Reset daily state
        if today != self._last_date:
            self._fired_today = False
            self._last_date   = today
        
        if self._fired_today:
            return None
        
        # Only in entry window 03:00–05:30 ET
        if not (dtime(3, 0) <= now.time() <= dtime(5, 30)):
            return None
        
        # Compute range from 02:00–02:59 bars
        range_bars = _bars_between(bars_1m, 2, 0, 2, 59)
        if len(range_bars) < 1:
            return None
        
        rng_high  = max(b['high'] for b in range_bars)
        rng_low   = min(b['low']  for b in range_bars)
        rng_width = rng_high - rng_low
        if rng_width <= 0:
            return None
        
        # Get latest bar close
        entry_bars = _bars_between(bars_1m, 3, 0, 5, 30)
        if not entry_bars:
            return None
        
        latest_close = entry_bars[-1]['close']
        stop_pts   = rng_width
        target_pts = self.RR_RATIO * rng_width
        
        direction = None
        if latest_close > rng_high:
            direction = 'long'
        elif latest_close < rng_low:
            direction = 'short'
        
        if direction is None:
            return None
        
        self._fired_today = True
        return {
            'direction'  : direction,
            'stop_pts'   : stop_pts,
            'target_pts' : target_pts,
            'instrument' : 'MCLJ6',
            'exit_time'  : dtime(9, 0),
            'rationale'  : f"London ORB breakout {direction} | range={rng_width:.3f} | "
                           f"H={rng_high:.3f} L={rng_low:.3f}",
        }


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STRATEGY 3: Nat Gas Asian Continuation + EMA Filter
# Symbol: MNGJ6
# Hours: Enter 00:00–00:05 ET, Exit 06:00 ET
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class NatGasAsianEMASignal:
    """
    Asian session continuation for Nat Gas with EMA(8)/EMA(21) alignment filter.
    Fires at 00:00–00:05 ET only when both Asian trend and hourly EMA agree.
    """
    SL_RATIO  = 0.4   # stop = 0.4× Asian range
    TP_RATIO  = 1.5   # target = 1.5× Asian range (conservative; raise to 2.5 when confident)
    MIN_RANGE = 0.03  # minimum Asian range in price units (e.g., $0.03/mmbtu)

    def signal(self, bars_1m: list, bars_1h: list, news_day: bool = False) -> dict | None:
        """
        Returns signal dict or None.
        
        Args:
            bars_1m:  1-minute bars (current session, covering Asian window)
            bars_1h:  1-hour bars (last 30+ bars for EMA calculation)
            news_day: skip if EIA Thursday or FOMC/CPI
            
        Returns:
            signal dict or None
        """
        if news_day:
            return None
        
        now = _now_et()
        if not (dtime(0, 0) <= now.time() <= dtime(0, 5)):
            return None
        
        # Asian session stats
        asian = _bars_between(bars_1m, 20, 0, 23, 59)
        if len(asian) < 10:
            return None
        
        asia_open  = asian[0]['open']
        asia_close = asian[-1]['close']
        asia_high  = max(b['high'] for b in asian)
        asia_low   = min(b['low']  for b in asian)
        asia_range = asia_high - asia_low
        
        if asia_range < self.MIN_RANGE:
            return None
        
        asia_bullish = asia_close > asia_open
        
        # EMA filter on 1-hour bars
        if len(bars_1h) < 21:
            return None
        closes_1h = [b['close'] for b in bars_1h]
        ema8  = _ema(closes_1h, 8)
        ema21 = _ema(closes_1h, 21)
        if ema8 is None or ema21 is None:
            return None
        
        ema_bullish = ema8 > ema21
        ema_bearish = ema8 < ema21
        
        # Require EMA alignment with Asian trend
        if asia_bullish and not ema_bullish:
            return None
        if not asia_bullish and not ema_bearish:
            return None
        
        sl_pts = self.SL_RATIO * asia_range
        tp_pts = self.TP_RATIO * asia_range
        
        return {
            'direction'  : 'long' if asia_bullish else 'short',
            'stop_pts'   : sl_pts,
            'target_pts' : tp_pts,
            'instrument' : 'MNGJ6',
            'exit_time'  : dtime(6, 0),
            'rationale'  : f"NG Asian {'bull' if asia_bullish else 'bear'} "
                           f"range={asia_range:.3f} | EMA8={ema8:.3f} EMA21={ema21:.3f}",
        }


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# UNIFIED OVERNIGHT SIGNAL DISPATCHER
# Call this from your main bot loop
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Instantiate once at module level
_gold_signal  = GoldAsianContinuationSignal()
_crude_signal = CrudeOilLondonORBSignal()
_ng_signal    = NatGasAsianEMASignal()

def check_overnight_signals(
    bars_by_symbol: dict,   # {'MGCJ6': [bars_1m], 'MCLJ6': [bars_1m], ...}
    bars_1h_by_symbol: dict,  # {'MNGJ6': [bars_1h], ...} (only NG needs 1h)
    news_day_symbols: set = None,  # {'MGCJ6', 'MCLJ6'} — symbols to skip today
) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Check all overnight signals and return list of actionable signals.
    
    Call every minute from your main asyncio loop.
    Returns list of signal dicts (can be empty).
    
    Example bot integration:
        signals = check_overnight_signals(
            bars_by_symbol={'MGCJ6': gold_bars, 'MCLJ6': crude_bars, 'MNGJ6': ng_bars},
            bars_1h_by_symbol={'MNGJ6': ng_bars_1h},
            news_day_symbols=get_news_day_symbols(),  # your news calendar function
        )
        for sig in signals:
            await place_trade(sig['instrument'], sig['direction'],
                              stop_pts=sig['stop_pts'], target_pts=sig['target_pts'],
                              exit_time=sig['exit_time'])
    """
    if news_day_symbols is None:
        news_day_symbols = set()
    
    signals = []
    
    # Gold: Asian Continuation
    if 'MGCJ6' in bars_by_symbol:
        sig = _gold_signal.signal(
            bars_by_symbol['MGCJ6'],
            news_day=('MGCJ6' in news_day_symbols)
        )
        if sig:
            signals.append(sig)
    
    # Crude: London ORB
    if 'MCLJ6' in bars_by_symbol:
        sig = _crude_signal.signal(
            bars_by_symbol['MCLJ6'],
            news_day=('MCLJ6' in news_day_symbols)
        )
        if sig:
            signals.append(sig)
    
    # Nat Gas: Asian + EMA
    if 'MNGJ6' in bars_by_symbol:
        sig = _ng_signal.signal(
            bars_by_symbol['MNGJ6'],
            bars_1h=bars_1h_by_symbol.get('MNGJ6', []),
            news_day=('MNGJ6' in news_day_symbols)
        )
        if sig:
            signals.append(sig)
    
    return signals


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# NEWS DAY HELPER — Extend with your calendar source
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

WEEKLY_BLACKOUTS = {
    2: {'MCLJ6'},   # Wednesday: EIA Petroleum Inventory → skip crude
    3: {'MNGJ6'},   # Thursday:  EIA Nat Gas Storage    → skip nat gas
}

def get_news_day_symbols(extra_blackouts: set = None) -> set:
    """
    Returns set of instrument symbols to skip today based on known calendar.
    Extend this with FOMC/CPI/NFP dates from your news calendar.
    
    Args:
        extra_blackouts: additional symbols to skip (e.g., from FOMC calendar check)
    """
    now = _now_et()
    dow = now.weekday()  # 0=Mon, 2=Wed, 3=Thu
    
    symbols = set(WEEKLY_BLACKOUTS.get(dow, set()))
    
    if extra_blackouts:
        symbols |= extra_blackouts
    
    return symbols
```

---

## Caveats & Forward Testing Plan

1. **Data quality:** yfinance 1-hour bars are end-of-day adjusted and may not reflect exact overnight prices. The strategy logic is sound, but live execution with 1-minute bars should be monitored for the first 2–3 weeks.

2. **Slippage:** No slippage was modeled. For micro futures with 1–2 tick spreads, add 1–2 ticks to each SL for realistic performance.

3. **Sample size:** 33–52 trades over 90 days. Minimum recommended sample for statistical confidence is 100+ trades. Monitor for 30 days live before full deployment.

4. **Nat Gas caution:** Despite the high PF (2.49–2.64), Nat Gas has extreme volatility during weather events (winter demand spikes, storage surprises). During January–February (polar vortex season), consider halving position size or skipping.

5. **Recommend forward test order:**
   - Week 1–2: Paper trade all three strategies, log all signals
   - Week 3: Enable Gold only (most consistent, highest win rate)
   - Week 4: Enable Crude (steady PF across all RR ratios)
   - Week 5+: Enable Nat Gas (monitor the low win rate tolerance)

---

## Files in This Research Directory

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `backtest_overnight.py`  | Initial 4-strategy × 5-instrument backtest |
| `backtest_refined.py`    | Parameter sweep on top 3 strategies |
| `backtest_results.json`  | Raw results from initial backtest |
| `refined_results.json`   | Best params from refined backtest |
| `overnight_strategy_report.md` | This report |

research_reports.name: Overnight Oos Report
research_reports.content:
# Overnight Strategy OOS Validation Report
**Generated:** 2026-02-25 14:44
**Data:** GC/CL/NG 1h bars (Oct 2023 – Feb 2026, ~29 months)
**Walk-Forward:** 12 windows × 8-week train + 4-week test
**Costs:** 1-tick slippage ×2 + $1.02 commission = **$3.02/trade**
**Simulation:** Bar-by-bar SL/TP detection (correct ordering)

---

## ⚠️ Important Caveats

1. **1h proxy data** — original strategy uses 1-min bars for precise SL/TP. 1h bars mean:
   - SL/TP detection has ±1h resolution → real results will differ
   - London ORB entry detection with 1h bars is conservative (requires hourly close outside range)
   - Asian session range uses only 4 hourly bars (20:00, 21:00, 22:00, 23:00 ET)
2. **Forward contract roll bias** — yfinance continuous proxy data has roll adjustments
3. **No news filter** — EIA, FOMC, CPI days not excluded (real bot filters these)

## Executive Summary

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gold (MGCJ6) | ASIAN_CONT | 40.7% | 1.27 | 44.6% | 1.19 | 0.74 | ⚠️ MARGINAL |
